L1.area: Real-time connection handling and HTTP upgrade infrastructure

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where authentication callbacks configured for upgraded persistent connections are never invoked during the connection establishment process. This allows unauthenticated clients to access protected endpoints. Downstream handlers that expect authenticated user context data to be populated receive nil values instead, which may additionally cause a panic and denial of service.

L3.area: WebSocket connection upgrade and per-request authentication middleware
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists where authentication callbacks configured for WebSocket endpoint handlers are never invoked during the connection upgrade process. This allows unauthenticated clients to establish WebSocket connections to protected endpoints, completely bypassing the intended authentication controls. Handlers that attempt to access the expected authenticated user data encounter nil values, potentially causing a panic and denial of service.

ground_truth.reason: The socketHandler function does not call the configured AuthenticateMethod before upgrading WebSocket connections, allowing unauthenticated access to protected endpoints and leaving UserData nil.
